mechanics.text:
SKILLS
CAREERS" This iS catchall category for an infinite number of special skills related t0 professions_ Since there is a endless supply of unique professions in the Battle Tech universe this generic category applies to them all rather than listing them separately: From Judge t0 Librarian, the possibilities are endless_ Of course_ certain professions require additiona skills. For example doctor needs more than Career /Doctor in order t0 practice medicine would also need First Aid MedTech Surgery and other skills In many cases_ the occupational fields 52) offer guidance on these requirements The final arbiter these cases is always the gamemaster; As rule 0t thumb there is an appropriate skill to profession on the Skill List, character must have it to be considered professional.
Additionally; the Computers Skill allows player t0 hack into computer systems by making Computers Skill Check: If system operator (sysop} is online when character attempts t0 hack into system; an Opposed Test is instead made using the character' = and sysop'$ Computers Skills If the sysop wins the would-be hacker is forced offline ad any further attempts on the same system receive +2 modifier: the hacking character wins he breaks into the system; but the sysop may be aware of the intrusion. If the hacker' = Margin of Success exceeds the sysop' MoS by three or more, the intrusion goes unnoticed Otherwise_ the sysop is aware of the breach and may shut down the system in 106 turns Once inside system; hacking character can act freely; though complex tasks may require Action Checks (bad failures and fumbles may alert sysop at the gamemaster'= discretion). system IS being shut down; hacking character can make one Computer Check per round in a attempt to extract data; these attempts suffer pressure modifier of +1 in addition to all other applicable modifiers_ Gamemasters should note that only the most sensitive data is continually monitored by live sysops_
CLIMBING The Climbing Skill enables characters to scale natural and manmade obstacles Various conditions affect the difficulty of climb Simulate these variables by applying TN modifiers Climbing Action Checks based on the guidelines found on the Target Number Modifiers Table (p 17); abundant handholds might offer Modifier; while slippery rocks r high winds would inflict +1 +3 Modifiers worse depending the severity Of the weather; failed Climbing Check results in fall: See the Falling sec tion in Combat; 120 , for rules on damage caused by falling
CRYPTOGRAPHY Characters use the Cryptography Skill encode and decode messages. When character encodes message_ the character" Margin of Success becomes the difficulty modifier for anyone attempting to decode the message Characters may voluntarily lower their Margins of Success to enable the mes sage recipient t0 decode the message more easily: failed roll means the message has not been properly encoded and will be unintelligible_ Decoding message also requires successiul Cryptography Action Check: When using computerized code key; the decoding process requires minute per page Using manual key extends the required time to 10 minutes per page Decoding without key requires 206 2 hours per page decoding times are divided by the decoding character's Margin of Success (the time Is not reduced the Margin of Success is Or 11.
COMMSICONVENTIONAL Characters use the Comms/Conventional Skill when oper ating communications equipment other than hyperpulse gener- ators This skill enables character t0 jam an opponent" com munications Or defeat a opponent" attempts (0 jam his own communications either case_ the character makes Opposed Test   against the   opponent'$ Comms Conventional Skill: This skill also includes knowledge of listening devices and other covert surveillance equipment,
COMMSMPG The Comms/HPG Skill allows characters to send interstel: Iar or interplanetary communications via hyperpulse generator (HPG). successful Comms/HPG Action Check means that such communication attempt succeeds_ This skill quite rare . though more and more people outside ComStar are Ilearn ing Many Clan technicians are trained in the operation of HPG stations as well as other similar technologies See 182 for description of HPGs
DEMOLITIONS character uses the Demolitions Skill to arm, disarm ad recognize explosive devices. Modifiers for Action Checks t0 arm explosive devices are based on number of factors ranging from the sensitivity of the explosive used to the complexity of the device detonation mechanism; Truly complex devices may require the use 0f other skills such as Computers_ Electronics or Security Systems_ The character arming the device sets the difficulty modifier to the Demolitions Action Check used t0 arm the device_ (A Iow modifier makes arming the device easy but also enables other characters to disarm the device easily: ) If the arming Action Check succeeds the device has been armed If the roll fails_ the arming attempt fails and the character must make second Demolitions Action Check immediately; using the same modi
COMPUTERS The Computers Skill gives the character working knowledge of computer systems_ basic operations and programming When attempting complex task such as searching large database Or writing new program the character must make an Action Check: The gamemaster assigns difficulty modifier appropriate to the complexity of the task, the time available to complete and the hardware and software the character is using:
98
